samples.gvd:
module RAM(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [2:0] write_addr,
    input [5:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [2:0] read_addr,
    output reg [5:0] read_data
);
parameter WIDTH = 6;
parameter DEPTH = 8;
reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        for (int i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i++) begin
            RAM[i] <= 6'b000000;
        end
    end
    else if (write_en) begin
        RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
    end
end
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end
    else if (read_en) begin
        read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
    end
    else begin
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a 8*6 RAM array.

reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];

Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the RAM array at !rst_n.

if (!rst_n) begin for (int i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i++) begin RAM[i] <= 6'b000000; end end

Write data at the positive edge of the clock signal when write_en is 1.

if (write_en) begin RAM[write_addr] <= write_data; end

Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the read_data register at !rst_n.

if (!rst_n) begin read_data <= 6'b000000; end

Read data at the positive edge of the clock signal when read_en is 1.

else if (read_en) begin read_data <= RAM[read_addr]; end

Reset the read_data register when read_en is not 1.

else begin read_data <= 6'b000000; end
